evidence_excerpt: Imaging and discharge note indicate disease growth: "your cancer has grown"; CT shows "interval increase in size" of left supraclavicular mass with bony destruction, enlarging lung lesion, and T5 lytic metastasis with epidural encroachment.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Presenting with "worsening neck pain"; mass eroding bone "most likely the cause of your pain"; oxycodone 5 mg q4h PRN prescribed.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: ED/Admission: "worsening" right-sided neck/shoulder/arm pain with CT showing interval increase of left supraclavicular mass and enlarging lung lesion.

CT/MRI: "Lytic metastatic lesion involving the T5 vertebral body, now with mild encroachment into the epidural space, mildly narrowing the spinal canal"; partial bony destruction of left clavicular head.
Discharge instructions: "you received chemotherapy and started radiation therapy while in the hospital. You will continue radiation therapy daily when you return home."
Functional decline at discharge: requires assistive devices (walker/cane), plus durable medical equipment (wheelchair, hospital bed) ordered.

notes_on_uncertainty: Long-term follow-up after discharge is not documented; dates are redacted. Outcome and quality-of-life categories are inferred from objective imaging of progression and the need for assistive devices and ongoing radiation, using hospital admission as the index time.
